title: Ignore Rules, Attributes, Line Endings, and Repository Content · Changed-History Lab
summary: Keep generated output, local configuration, binaries, and secrets out of history while preserving cross-platform text behavior. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a content-boundary and normalization policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.
Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.
Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence.

What must be established before implementation?
